title: Generate requirements for a compact air purifier for a bedro
stakeholder_needs: Generate requirements for a compact air purifier for a bedroom. It should cover at least 25 square meters, operate below 35 dB in sleep mode, and comply with EU safety and environmental regulations.
analysis: Key stakeholders include: (1) End users (bedroom occupants) who prioritize quiet operation, effective purification for allergies/odors, low drafts/light at night, easy use and maintenance, and acceptable size/aesthetics; (2) Purchasers (consumers/retailers) who care about price, energy use, filter replacement cost/availability, and warranty; (3) Regulatory bodies/EU market access requiring CE-marking compliance with applicable safety (electrical, EMC) and environmental rules (RoHS, REACH, WEEE, Ecodesign/energy labeling where applicable); (4) Manufacturer/service teams needing clear performance test methods (coverage/CADR, noise), manufacturability, supply constraints (filters, fans), and serviceability; (5) Install/use environment constraints typical of bedrooms (night use, small spaces, children/pets). Derived requirements should therefore specify measurable purification performance for ≥25 m², acoustics <35 dB in sleep mode with defined test conditions, safety-by-design, materials/chemicals compliance, end-of-life obligations, and usability/maintenance requirements (filter life indicators, easy replacement).
clarification_questions: What room conditions should define the 25 m² coverage requirement (assumed ceiling height, target air changes per hour or CADR, and particulate size standard to test against, e.g., ISO 16890/GB/T/ANSI AHAM)?

What contaminants are in scope (PM2.5/PM10, pollen/allergens, VOCs/odors, smoke) and are any filtration technologies mandated or excluded (HEPA grade, activated carbon, ionizer/ozone allowed or explicitly prohibited)?

Any constraints on form factor and features (maximum dimensions/weight, allowable night lights/display brightness, smart connectivity/app, budget/energy limit, and filter replacement interval targets)?

table.title: DFMEA - Compact Bedroom Air Purifier (EU Market)
rows: Provide safe energy storage for portable/cordless variant; no fire/venting in normal/abuse use
Fire/smoke in bedroom; injury; property damage; product recall
Cell internal short; overcharge; overheating due to blocked airflow; mechanical damage
Cell supplier qualification; BMS with OVP/UVP/OCP/OTP; pack spacing/insulation; UL1642/IEC62133 component selection
BMS fault logging; end-of-line insulation/hipot; abuse test (overcharge, crush); thermal imaging during validation
Add independent secondary protector (CID/PTC + fuse); improve thermal path; add pack venting direction away from user; implement conservative charge profile
Prototype BMS includes OTP and pack fuse; supplier PPAP pending
Design for safe failure and vent path; require dual-layer protection for Li-ion in bedroom products
If product is mains-only, treat as N/A in variant matrix but keep DFMEA row for future cordless SKU
Maintain capacity and runtime over life
Customer dissatisfaction; returns; increased cost; failure to meet advertised performance
High temp operation; high charge voltage; poor cell matching; deep discharge
Cell spec with cycle-life data; charge voltage limit; pack balancing; thermal design review
Capacity EOL test sampling; BMS cycle count and SOH algorithm; incoming cell IR screening
Lower max SOC in sleep/idle; add better matching criteria; improve airflow around pack; update runtime claim with margin
Initial cells meet datasheet; no SOH algorithm implemented yet
Runtime claims must include aging margin; define SOH thresholds for service guidance
Coordinate with marketing to avoid overstated runtime claims
Provide required airflow/CADR while ensuring reliability
Loss of airflow; reduced purification; possible overheating; noise increase
Dust ingress; inadequate bearing spec; imbalance; lubricant dry-out
Sealed bearing selection; rotor balance spec; dust pre-screen; supplier endurance testing
End-of-line current/noise check; HALT endurance; vibration measurement
Upgrade to higher L10 bearing; add dust labyrinth; implement predictive noise/current drift screening in production
Supplier provides 500h endurance data; no dust endurance yet
Dust + endurance combined tests reveal early failures; include particulate exposure in validation
Tie endurance to worst-case bedroom dust/pet hair exposure
Meet sleep-mode acoustic requirement (<35 dB(A)) and low vibration
Excessive noise or tonal peaks in sleep mode
Sleep disturbance; negative reviews; returns; non-compliance with spec
Blade pass tone; resonance with housing; PWM switching noise; poor isolation mounts
Acoustic design review; modal analysis; soft mounts; optimized fan curve; PWM frequency selection
Anechoic/quiet-room noise test; EOL noise test with spectral limits
Add acoustic foam/liner; redesign grille to reduce turbulence; shift PWM >20 kHz; add tighter fan balance tolerances
Preliminary SPL 37 dB(A) at 1 m in quiet room; tonal issue at 400 Hz
Tonal noise often drives complaints even when dB(A) meets target; include tonality metric in DV plan
Define test: 1 m, free-field, sleep mode, new filter, 23°C, 50%RH
Deliver rated CADR and coverage for ≥25 m²
Reduced purification performance; cannot achieve coverage; allergen/odor persistence
Poor duct geometry; leaks at joints; blocked inlet by bedding/curtains; undersized fan
CFD optimization; inlet guard; gasketed joints; airflow margin in sizing
CADR test per ISO 29463/GB/T 18801 method; pressure drop measurement; smoke visualization
Add inlet blockage detection (pressure/current); enlarge inlet area; add better sealing features; publish clearance requirements
CADR margin only 5% with current grille; no blockage detection
Include real-use blockage scenarios in DV (bedside, wall proximity, curtains)
Specify installation clearance ≥200 mm from walls/soft furnishings
Remove particulates and odors; maintain performance over life
Particle/odor breakthrough; allergy symptoms; reputation damage
Poor pleat bonding; shipping damage; improper user installation; frame warp
Robust filter frame design; keyed installation; incoming QC; packaging drop test
EOL airflow/particle penetration sampling; visual inspection; user mis-installation test
Add pre-filter screen; strengthen pleat adhesive spec; add filter latch with positive lock; add user instructions with diagrams
Current prototype has friction-fit only; no latch
Keyed, latched filters reduce bypass and service errors; design for tool-less but foolproof replacement
Ensure replacement filters are widely available to reduce off-brand fit issues
Indicate filter life and maintain airflow
Reduced CADR; increased noise; higher power draw; user dissatisfaction
High dust/pet hair; small filter area; poor pre-filter; humid environment
Filter sizing with margin; washable pre-filter; filter-change indicator algorithm using runtime/pressure
Pressure sensor validation; power/current trend monitoring; user field feedback
Increase filter surface area; add better pre-filter; add pressure sensor for real clog detection; define recommended replacement interval by environment
Indicator currently time-based only; no pressure sensing
Time-only indicators underperform across households; use pressure or airflow estimation for accuracy
Provide user guidance for pets/smoking/high-dust rooms
Provide safe structure, finger protection, stability and aesthetics
Housing crack/break leading to sharp edges or access to live parts
Injury; electrical shock risk; fails safety compliance; returns
Drop/impact; brittle plastic; weak screw bosses; stress concentration near grille
Material selection (UL94 as needed); ribbing; drop-test design; DFMA torque specs
Drop/impact test; EOL visual inspection; torque audit
Add fillets/ribs; switch to tougher polymer; add internal barrier to maintain IP2X even if outer grille cracks
Boss cracking observed in 1/10 pilot builds
Design for drop robustness in bedroom scenarios (nightstand falls)
Ensure compliance with EN 60335 mechanical strength clauses
Maintain airtight separation to prevent bypass and rattles
Gasket compression set / leakage over time
CADR reduction; increased noise/rattle; dust leakage
Wrong material; high temperature; poor tolerance stack; over-compression
Gasket material spec (EPDM/silicone); compression target; tolerance analysis; assembly fixtures
Leak test sampling; acoustic rattle check; field return analysis
Add controlled compression stops; select low-compression-set silicone; implement 100% leak test at EOL for critical joints
No leak test in current line plan; gasket material not finalized
Tolerance + gasket design must be co-optimized; add compression limiters early
Provide safe power conversion and meet EU EMC/safety
Fire hazard; shock hazard; unit shutdown; compliance failure
Undersized components; blocked vents; high ambient; poor thermal interface; counterfeit parts
Use certified PSU (EN 61558/62368 as applicable); derating; thermal simulation; supplier audit
Thermal rise test; EOL functional test; incoming inspection; burn-in sampling
Increase thermal margin; add thermal fuse/NTC; add airflow path isolation from PSU; implement anti-counterfeit controls
Thermal test at 40°C not yet executed
Prefer certified external adapter to reduce enclosure thermal risk; verify EMC with worst-case cable
Ensure creepage/clearance meets EN 60335/62368 as selected
Meet EMC emission/immunity for CE marking
Cannot CE mark; sales block; redesign cost; customer interference complaints
Poor filtering; noisy motor drive; cable acting as antenna; grounding issues
EMC design rules; filters/chokes; shield strategy; PCB layout review; pre-compliance scans
Pre-scan in chamber; full EN 55014/EN 61000 testing; firmware mitigation for transients
Add common-mode choke; improve PCB return paths; add shielding can; adjust motor PWM edge rates; perform iterative pre-scan early
No pre-scan performed; motor driver layout first pass
Schedule pre-compliance before tooling freeze; motor PWM often dominates emissions
Include immunity (EFT/ESD/surge) with functional criteria defined
Allow easy use at night; low light; correct mode indication
UI too bright or confusing; sleep mode not actually quiet/dim
LEDs not dimmed enough; poor labeling; firmware state mismatch; no child-lock
Human factors review; brightness control; clear icons; child-lock requirement; firmware state machine review
User testing; EOL UI functional test; firmware unit tests
Add true night mode: <1 cd/m² indicator; auto-dim; long-press lock; ensure sleep mode caps fan RPM and disables beeps
Current LEDs fixed brightness; no lock feature
Night usability drives bedroom satisfaction; define measurable luminance and acoustic constraints per mode
Add option to fully turn off display while maintaining safety alerts
Prevent finger access to moving fan and internal hazards (IP2X)
Grille opening allows finger contact with fan
Large grille apertures; weak grille; deformation under load
Design to IP2X probe; grille stiffness; safety review to EN 60335
IP probe test; mechanical load test; inspection of grille dimensions
Reduce aperture size; add internal secondary guard; add structural ribs to prevent deflection
Initial industrial design uses large aesthetic openings
Always validate with standardized test probes, not just CAD checks
Ensure airflow impact accounted for when shrinking apertures
Comply with EU environmental regulations and documentation for CE/market access
Sales stop; fines; recalls; reputational harm
Uncontrolled material changes; supplier substitution; lack of declarations; ink/adhesive contamination
Approved materials list; supplier declarations; contract clauses; incoming compliance checks
Document review; random XRF screening; SVHC database checks; audits
Implement material compliance management system; require full material disclosure (FMD); add periodic XRF per lot; label plastics for recycling
Supplier provides RoHS statement only; no FMD yet
Declarations alone are insufficient; enforce traceability and change notification
Include WEEE marking and producer registration per target EU countries
Maintain safe operation under faults; ensure correct fan control and protections
Firmware crash or control loop runaway
Fan stuck at high speed (noise) or stops (no purification); possible overheating without safeguards
Unhandled exceptions; watchdog disabled; memory corruption; OTA update interruption
Coding standards; watchdog; failsafe default; CRC checks; safe bootloader; FMEA-based fault handling
HIL testing; fault injection; watchdog test; EOL firmware version check
Implement dual-bank firmware update; add independent hardware thermal cut-off; expand fault coverage and logging; define safe state per fault
Watchdog planned but not enabled in current build
Bedroom products need predictable behavior; safe state should prioritize fire safety and quiet alerts
Add service log export for warranty triage

messages.content: Generate requirements for a compact air purifier for a bedroom. It should cover at least 25 square meters, operate below 35 dB in sleep mode, and comply with EU safety and environmental regulations.

Key stakeholders include: (1) End users (bedroom occupants) who prioritize quiet operation, effective purification for allergies/odors, low drafts/light at night, easy use and maintenance, and acceptable size/aesthetics; (2) Purchasers (consumers/retailers) who care about price, energy use, filter replacement cost/availability, and warranty; (3) Regulatory bodies/EU market access requiring CE-marking compliance with applicable safety (electrical, EMC) and environmental rules (RoHS, REACH, WEEE, Ecodesign/energy labeling where applicable); (4) Manufacturer/service teams needing clear performance test methods (coverage/CADR, noise), manufacturability, supply constraints (filters, fans), and serviceability; (5) Install/use environment constraints typical of bedrooms (night use, small spaces, children/pets). Derived requirements should therefore specify measurable purification performance for ≥25 m², acoustics <35 dB in sleep mode with defined test conditions, safety-by-design, materials/chemicals compliance, end-of-life obligations, and usability/maintenance requirements (filter life indicators, easy replacement).
